mission.title: Mission & vision
mission.body:
I am an HCI researcher and technology-based artist interested in how taste perception can be represented, decomposed, and reimagined through computational systems. As an amateur cocktail maker and home cook, I am drawn to how emerging technologies might help people better understand, record, and create with food and flavor.

Food is never only a matter of taste; it is also connected to nutrition, health, ecology, memory, labor, and cultural identity. Yet large flavor knowledge systems often require heavy resources and centralized maintenance, while recipe-driven pairing tools can be limited in supporting artistic originality, situated experience, and speculative imagination. This tension leads me to ask whether we need different ways to produce technological knowledge about flavor—ways that can carry human heuristics about taste into creation, documentation, and sharing rather than replacing embodied expertise.

My work explores computational systems that help people articulate personal inspirations, preserve subjective flavor experience, and collaborate through workshops where knowledge emerges through shared making, tasting, discussion, and reflection. I see flavor-based interaction as a meaningful contribution to HCI and human–food interaction: interfaces and archives that bridge personal memory, cultural context, and digital tools, so more people can participate in how flavor is understood and created.

about.title: About me
about.body:
I am Po-Yu Chen (pillow)—an HCI researcher, creative technologist, and artist based in Taipei, Taiwan.

I earned a B.F.A. in Art and Technology from National Tsing Hua University (NTHU). I am now a second-year master's student in the Graduate Institute of Networking and Multimedia (GINM) at National Taiwan University (NTU), advised by Professor Lung-Pen Cheng.

My practice spans human–food interaction, extended reality, and LLM-supported creative systems. Outside research, I enjoy home cooking and mixology as personal ways to explore flavor, memory, and culture.

Website: pillowlowchen.com

switchers.mainContent: 關於我 / 使命與願景

mission.title: 使命與願景
mission.body:
我是一名 HCI 研究者與科技藝術工作者，關注味覺感知如何透過計算系統被呈現、拆解與重新想像。作為業餘調酒師與家庭料理者，我對新技術如何幫助人們理解、記錄並創造飲食與風味充滿興趣。

食物從不只是味覺；它同時連結營養、健康、生態、記憶、勞動與文化認同。然而大型風味知識系統往往需要龐大資源與集中式維護，而以食譜為基礎的配對工具，有時也難以支持藝術原創性、情境化經驗與思辨想像。這促使我思考：我們是否需要另一種生產風味技術知識的方式——讓系統能承載人們關於味道的啟發式判斷，並帶入創作、記錄與分享，而非取代身體化的專業與日常經驗。

我的工作探索計算系統如何協助人們表達個人靈感、保存主觀風味經驗，並透過工作坊共同製作、品嚐、討論與反思而產生知識。我將風味互動視為對 HCI 與人類–食物互動的重要貢獻：讓介面與檔案連結個人記憶、文化脈絡與數位工具，使更多人能參與風味的理解與創造。

about.title: 關於我
about.body:
我是陳柏宇（pillow）——一位 HCI 研究者、創意科技工作者與藝術家，現居台灣台北。

我畢業於國立清華大學藝術與科技學系，取得藝術學士（B.F.A.）。目前就讀國立台灣大學網路與多媒體研究所（GINM）碩士二年級，指導教授為彭龍斌老師。

我的實踐涵蓋人類–食物互動、延展實境（XR），以及大型語言模型支援的創意系統。研究之外，我喜歡在家烹飪與調酒，作為探索風味、記憶與文化的個人方式。

網站：pillowlowchen.com
